home_p_41: Dubai Plus — The Complete Guide to Dubai
home_p_43: Dubai Plus is the premium planning experience for your trip to Dubai. Handpicked hotels, breathtaking attractions, transfers, SIM cards, restaurants and more — all accessible, organized, and ready to book. Plan your dream vacation with ease and style.
home_p_44: A wide selection of luxury hotels in Dubai — and Abu Dhabi, from the city center to the beachfront. Book directly via WhatsApp quickly and easily.
home_p_46: Burj Khalifa, yachts, Global Village, Desert Safari and more — unforgettable experiences with instant booking available.
home_p_48: Fast and reliable connectivity in Dubai. Get an international SIM card before your trip and stay connected throughout your journey.
home_p_50: A comfortable and organized ride from the airport to your hotel and back. Book a private transfer with experienced and courteous drivers for a seamless arrival experience.
home_p_52: Discover Dubai's finest culinary scene — from rooftop gourmet restaurants to unique and unforgettable dining experiences.

home_p_56: Three iconic experiences that define Dubai — from the ocean to the skies, from the desert to the summit
home_p_57: Step aboard a private yacht and sail along Dubai's breathtaking skyline. An unforgettable experience for couples, families, and special events — your own private sea.
home_p_58: Embark on a once-in-a-lifetime experience among Dubai's golden dunes. Camel riding, thrilling jeep rides, and a traditional Bedouin dinner under a canopy of stars.
home_p_59: Ascend to the top of the world's tallest structure and view Dubai from 555 meters. A panoramic view like no other — an experience that redefines the concept of heights.
home_p_61: We believe every trip to Dubai should feel like a premium experience from the very first moment. Dubai Plus offers you a single platform that connects you directly to hotels, attractions, and travel services — with no unnecessary middlemen and no hidden fees.

home_p_65: From airport transfers to a local SIM card — everything you need for a perfect trip, in one place, in Hebrew with personal service.

hotels_h2_3: Why book through us instead of on your own?
hotels_h2_4: Not sure which hotel is right for you?
hotels_p_25: We offer a curated selection of the finest luxury hotels in Dubai — from the glittering towers of the Marina to the lavish resorts of Palm Jumeirah. Every hotel is carefully chosen to ensure you an unforgettable stay.
hotels_p_26: Fast, direct booking via WhatsApp — no hidden fees, no waiting. The Dubai Plus team is available for any question and to provide personal assistance in choosing the right hotel for you at the best and most affordable price.
hotels_p_29: Every hotel is carefully chosen for you — breathtaking views, world-class service, and an ideal location for the perfect Dubai experience. Book directly via WhatsApp with a single click.
hotels_p_30: An innovative luxury hotel on the JBR waterfront — direct beach access, stunning infinity pools, a world-class spa, and chef restaurants with panoramic views of the Arabian Sea. Dubai's most vibrant atmosphere with premium five-star service.
hotels_p_32: The iconic hotel on the Palm Islands — infinity pools, a private beach, 25 restaurants and bars, and a lively, energetic atmosphere 24 hours a day. The most popular choice for luxury trips to Dubai with breathtaking views of the Palm Islands.
hotels_p_34: An avant-garde luxury tower in Business Bay with boundary-pushing artistic design. Stunning rooms and apartments with direct views of Burj Khalifa and the canal, an iconic rooftop pool, international chef restaurants, and a uniquely cosmopolitan atmosphere.
hotels_p_36: The Hilton brand offers a variety of properties across Dubai — Hilton Dubai Creek with views of Dubai Creek, Hilton Jumeirah on the beach, Hilton Garden Inn, and more. World-class service, prime locations, and global brand reliability.
hotels_p_38: One of the most iconic hotels in the world at the top of the Palm Islands — the massive Aquaventure water park, restaurants by celebrity chefs such as Nobu and Bread Street Kitchen, and an amazing aquarium. An unforgettable experience for the whole family.
hotels_p_40: In the heart of Dubai, adjacent to Dubai Mall and Burj Khalifa. Breathtaking views of the world's tallest tower and the dancing fountains. The perfect location for an urban Dubai experience with the highest level of service.
hotels_p_42: The winning combination of the French Riviera and the Palm Islands. An infinity pool facing the Arabian Sea, a quiet private beach, stunning design, and a secluded location — without sacrificing access to the main attractions. Ideal for couples.
hotels_p_44: Bold and energetic design, a panoramic rooftop pool with 360-degree views, world-class nightlife and entertainment. The W is the choice for those who want luxury with character — a unique and intense experience on the Palm Islands.
hotels_p_46: The only hotel in the world designed entirely by the Versace fashion house — every detail bears the mark of the iconic Italian brand. Manicured gardens, a private pool, and gourmet restaurants that have become legendary. An experience that is pure art.
hotels_p_48: A magnificent Arabian palace in the heart of Jumeirah Estate — water canals, traditional wooden boats, a stunning private beach, and a direct view of Burj Al Arab. A perfect blend of classic Arabian architecture and modern five-star service.
hotels_p_50: One of the most luxurious and grand hotels in Dubai — 13 gourmet restaurants, vast tropical gardens, indoor and outdoor pools, a world-class spa, and a stunning atrium lobby with waterfalls. A top-tier hospitality experience in the heart of the city, close to the airport.
hotels_p_52: A modern luxury hotel in the heart of Al Habtoor City — direct access to La Perle by Dragone, outdoor pools with views of Dubai Canal, elegant rooms with panoramic views, and international restaurants. An excellent location on Sheikh Zayed Road, close to all attractions.
hotels_p_54: A luxury hotel on the first line of Dubai Marina — breathtaking views of the marina and yachts, spacious suites in a luxury apartment style, an infinity pool, and private sun terraces. A perfect location in the heart of the Marina, steps from the promenade, restaurants, and malls.
hotels_p_56: All bookings are made directly via WhatsApp — no hidden fees, with personal guidance every step of the way.
hotels_p_57: Not just the best price — also personal service, full support, and availability throughout your entire vacation, so you can book with complete peace of mind and confidence.
hotels_p_58: We are available for you on WhatsApp — a question, a special request, a room upgrade, or any need that arises. No call centers, no waiting, no bureaucracy. One conversation with a human representative who knows Dubai inside out.
hotels_p_59: We guide you through the booking process in the simplest and fastest way possible. Instead of spending hours comparing prices and websites, we consolidate all the options for you and make sure your room is confirmed and secured before you board the plane.
hotels_p_60: Every traveler is different — families, couples, groups of friends. We listen to your needs and match the right hotel according to your budget, location, amenities, and atmosphere. From Palm Jumeirah to Downtown Dubai — we know every property and know what suits you.
hotels_p_61: We are here to help you choose the perfect hotel for your Dubai vacation — tailored to your budget, style, and expectations. Our team knows every hotel personally and is happy to provide individual advice.

attractions_h3_9: Burj Khalifa — Top of the World
attractions_p_54: Discover the most spectacular attractions in Dubai — from private yachts to desert safaris, from the Miracle Garden to Burj Khalifa. Each attraction is presented with a unique image and an easy booking option. We showcase the best places to visit, so you can plan your dream vacation with ease and style.
attractions_p_56: Discover the most breathtaking destinations in Dubai — from dramatic desert experiences to awe-inspiring sky-high heights. Every attraction has been carefully selected for you.
attractions_p_57: Sail the blue waters of the Marina on a luxurious private yacht — an unforgettable experience for any occasion.
attractions_p_59: Millions of flowers in vivid colors create stunning floral carpets — the largest natural garden in the world.
attractions_p_61: A cultural journey around the world in one place — pavilions, restaurants, entertainment and attractions from 90 countries.
attractions_p_63: A jeep adventure over golden sand dunes, camel riding, and a traditional Bedouin dinner under the stars.
attractions_p_65: Ascend to floors 124–148 and enjoy a 360-degree panoramic view — from the desert to the ocean. The observation experience of the world's tallest building, in the heart of Dubai.
attractions_p_67: Thrilling water slides, a lazy river and endless aquatic fun at the famous Aquaventure Waterpark on Palm Island.
attractions_p_69: Head up to The View at The Palm and enjoy a bird's-eye view of the iconic palm shape — one of the most breathtaking views in the world.
attractions_p_71: Luxury private yacht rental with a professional crew — 6 models to choose from, ranging from 34 to 60 feet. The perfect experience for families, couples and special events. ✔ The price shown is per one hour of rental.
attractions_p_73: A world-class experience on Yas Island — Ferrari World, Warner Bros. World and Yas Waterworld. World-class theme parks for the whole family, just a short drive from Dubai.
attractions_p_75: A luxurious cruise aboard a mega yacht — a festive dinner, live show and breathtaking views of Dubai's illuminated skyline. A magical and unforgettable evening.
attractions_p_77: A stunning acrobatic water show by Dragone — 65 artists, an underwater stage and breathtaking effects. A visual experience like no other in the heart of Dubai.
attractions_p_79: A unique driving experience in an amphibious vehicle that glides across the waters of Dubai Marina — an exciting and one-of-a-kind attraction. The perfect blend of adrenaline and scenery.
attractions_p_81: Soar above Dubai's stunning skyline — a panoramic helicopter flight over Burj Khalifa, Palm Island and the coastlines. A bird's-eye view like no other.
attractions_p_83: A spectacular dolphin show in Dubai — a magical experience for the whole family featuring intelligent dolphins, aquatic acrobatics and breathtaking effects.
attractions_p_85: A massive indoor ski resort in the heart of the desert — real snow, ski slopes, penguin encounters and a magical winter experience at Mall of the Emirates.
attractions_p_87: The world's largest indoor theme park — Marvel, Cartoon Network and dinosaur zones, thrilling roller coasters and attractions for the whole family.
attractions_p_89: The Dubai Balloon — a tethered hot air balloon above Atlantis, offering a stunning panoramic view of Palm Island and the bay from a height of 300 meters.
attractions_p_91: The takeoff is magical at dawn above the desert dunes — a breathtaking hot air balloon experience with an endless view of golden sands and first light.
attractions_p_93: Museum of the Future — an architectural masterpiece with interactive futuristic exhibitions. The most beautiful building in the world and an unforgettable experience.
attractions_p_95: A breathtaking observation experience from Souk Al Bahar Tower — an outdoor glass slide, a transparent glass walkway, and a stunning panoramic view of Dubai.
attractions_p_97: A magical digital universe of light, color, and sound — 12 interactive rooms in a breathtaking visual playground. An experience like no other.
attractions_p_99: Ride a jet ski on the blue waters of the bay with a view of Burj Khalifa — a thrilling extreme experience at high speed across the water. Suitable for individuals and couples.

restaurants_h2_5: Looking for the perfect restaurant in Dubai?
restaurants_p_22: From the coolest waterfront restaurants to gastronomic experiences you'll never forget — we've brought you the best spots in Dubai. Recommended restaurants for couples, families, and true luxury seekers. Because when you come to Dubai, even the dining needs to be an experience.
restaurants_p_23: Dubai's restaurants are so much more than food — they're a complete experience. We've selected four unique atmospheres for you, each telling a different story of this amazing city.
restaurants_p_24: Dubai's rooftop restaurants offer an unparalleled experience — illuminated tables, panoramic views of the Burj Khalifa and the bay, and international menus at Michelin-level quality. Ideal for special occasions, team evenings, and moments that are etched in memory.
restaurants_p_25: Dubai is full of restaurants that welcome families with children with open arms — diverse menus, spacious seating areas, and dishes from around the world. From the large mall restaurants to unique food experiences in the markets, there's something to make every child and adult smile.
restaurants_p_26: Imagine your feet in the sand, a gentle sea breeze, and fresh seafood on your plate — that's the beach dining atmosphere of Dubai. Beachfront restaurants along JBR, Palm Jumeirah and more offer a magical combination of scenery, quality food, and cocktails at sunset. An experience you simply can't forget.
restaurants_p_27: For those seeking the ultimate — Dubai's fine dining restaurants are works of art. Stunning interior design, Michelin-starred chefs, carefully curated tasting menus, and service that makes you feel like royalty. Advance reservations are a must, and the experience — beyond all expectations.
restaurants_p_28: Choose the package that best suits your trip. Home delivery or instant eSIM.
restaurants_p_29: A kosher restaurant under Chabad supervision, featuring Israeli home-style cooking with familiar flavors and a warm atmosphere.
restaurants_p_30: A luxury kosher restaurant at the Burj Al Arab hotel, featuring innovative fusion cuisine under OU supervision.
restaurants_p_31: A kosher restaurant with a rich Middle Eastern cuisine, perfectly located on the JBR Walk promenade.
restaurants_p_32: Traditional Shabbat and holiday meals, kosher catering for events. Address: Jumeirah 1.
restaurants_p_33: Kosher food delivery to your door, with a varied menu including Israeli and international dishes.
restaurants_p_34: Several recognized kosher certifications operate in Israel: OU, Chabad, and more. Always check the kashrut certificate.
restaurants_p_35: Kosher catering services are available for private and business events. Book in advance.
restaurants_p_36: It is recommended to reserve a table in advance, especially on weekends and holidays. Some restaurants offer delivery.
restaurants_p_37: Dubai offers a culinary world like no other — from Michelin-starred restaurants with views of the glittering skyline, to Mediterranean food markets filled with the scents of Eastern spices. Every meal here is an event in itself, combining stunning design, internationally acclaimed service, and menus that bring the finest flavors from around the world to a single table.
restaurants_p_38: Whether you choose to dine 120 floors above the clouds, on the deck of a private yacht, or in an intimate restaurant in the heart of the old city quarter — Dubai's culinary scene will ensure every bite stays with you forever.
restaurants_p_39: "In Dubai, the table is the stage — and the food is the grand performance."
restaurants_p_40: Our team knows Dubai's food scene inside and out — from gourmet restaurants with views of the Burj Khalifa to authentic local spots you won't find in any travel guide. Send us a message and we'll tailor restaurant recommendations to suit your taste, budget, and the atmosphere you're looking for.

attractions_h3_50: A Journey to the Heart of the Desert
attractions_p_101: A self-drive off-road experience on golden sand dunes — take the wheel and drive into the heart of the desert. An exciting route with breathtaking views.
attractions_p_103: Dubai's amazing children's city — a magical world where kids fulfill dreams and try out dozens of professions in a realistic environment. An educational and fun experience.
attractions_p_105: The largest and most magnificent water park in the world — wild slides, rushing rivers, a private beach, and endless water attractions. An unforgettable family experience.
attractions_p_107: Climb 240 meters to the breathtaking observation floor in the heart of the Palm Island — a 360° panoramic view of the island, Dubai's skyline, and the bay.
attractions_p_109: Dubai's iconic Frame — a glass observation bridge at 150 meters high connecting old and new Dubai. A breathtaking photography experience.
attractions_p_111: Enter a world where nothing is as it seems — dozens of interactive illusions, sensory rooms, and mirror mazes. Fun for all ages.
attractions_p_113: An experiential one-hour cruise in Dubai's magnificent marina — a panoramic view of the skyline, a magical atmosphere, and an accessible price. A nautical experience that will leave you wanting more.
attractions_p_115: Madame Tussauds Dubai — meet the world's biggest stars in perfect wax figures. Take selfies with celebrities, singers, and superheroes.
attractions_p_117: A magical interactive museum for children — discover, play, and learn in spaces of creativity, science, imagination, and nature. A whole world of enriching experiences for the entire family.
attractions_p_119: An Olympic ice skating experience in the heart of Dubai Mall — a massive ice rink, stunning lighting, and a magical atmosphere for the whole family. The perfect attraction to escape the heat.
attractions_p_121: Extreme speedboat rides on black boats in Dubai — a thrilling experience on the water with a breathtaking view of the skyline. Adrenaline, speed, and style.
attractions_p_123: A guided tour on iconic yellow boats — a unique sailing experience in Dubai with a professional guide. Cruise along the stunning coastline and discover Dubai from a different angle.
attractions_p_125: A romantic cruise on love boats in Dubai — a magical couples' experience on the water with an intimate atmosphere. Sail at sunset and create sweet memories in the heart of the marina.
attractions_p_127: Float in the air with an indoor skydiving experience — iFly Dubai offers a free-fall simulation in a vertical wind tunnel. A safe and thrilling extreme experience for the whole family.
attractions_p_129: Rise above the water with a flyboard — a breathtaking water sports experience in Dubai. Powerful water jets launch you into the air and the view of the skyline is simply mind-blowing. Pure adrenaline.
attractions_p_131: Walk on the edge of a skyscraper — the Sky Views Edge Walk experience offers a thrilling outdoor walk on the rim of the Sky Views Tower at a dizzying height. A breathtaking 360° view of Dubai from above.
attractions_p_133: Dine 50 meters above the ground — Dinner in the Sky Dubai is a luxurious dining experience floating in the open air. A giant crane lifts you and your table to the sky with a breathtaking panoramic view.
attractions_p_135: A luxurious limousine ride experience in Dubai — VIP service with a dedicated driver, a lavish vehicle, and perfect comfort. Perfect for special events, receptions, and traveling in style.
attractions_p_137: A luxurious and kosher desert safari experience — jeep rides over dunes, camel riding, a lavish kosher dinner under the open sky. An unforgettable experience with full kosher observance.
attractions_p_139: Embark on a unique desert adventure in Abu Dhabi — a thrilling off-road drive among giant dunes, breathtaking views, and an authentic experience in the heart of the Arabian desert. Suitable for families, couples, and groups.
attractions_p_141: Soar above Abu Dhabi on a luxury helicopter flight — a breathtaking view of the palaces, islands, stunning skyline, and blue bay. A once-in-a-lifetime experience.
attractions_p_143: An enchanting tropical forest in the heart of Dubai — hundreds of birds, butterflies, and exotic animals in a covered natural environment. A green and refreshing experience for the whole family.
attractions_p_145: Imagine yourself on the deck of a private yacht, gliding quietly against Dubai's skyline — the Burj Khalifa standing tall behind you, the sun sinking into the bay in shades of gold and red. Renting a yacht in Dubai is not just a sea trip — it's a moment etched in memory forever. Choose between a magical hour or a full panoramic cruise, and let us take care of everything else.
attractions_p_146: When the sun touches the tips of the golden dunes, the world seems to stand still. Dubai's desert tour is one of the rarest tourism experiences — a combination of adrenaline, tranquility, and breathtaking beauty. A 4x4 ride through the dunes, a traditional Bedouin dinner, belly dancing, and lantern lights under the stars — all of this awaits you just a short drive from the city.
attractions_p_147: Whatever attraction you've chosen — a private yacht, a desert safari, a view from the Burj Khalifa tower — we're here to arrange a complete and personalized experience for you. Get in touch and let's plan together the trip you've always dreamed of.

blog_dubai_shopping_guide_p_10: Dubai is not just a destination for adrenaline seekers or luxury vacationers — it's a true paradise for shoppers of all kinds. From massive malls offering every brand imaginable, through traditional markets full of character, to hidden designer boutiques — this city knows how to deliver. But with so many options, it's very easy to get scattered and find yourself running around with no direction. In this guide, we'll learn how to plan the perfect shopping experience, save precious time, and enjoy every moment.
blog_dubai_shopping_guide_p_11: Dubai's magic lies in its ability to blend old and new, traditional and modern, and shopping is the perfect example of this. You can start your morning at the most magnificent mall in the world, continue to an authentic spice market, and end at a hidden concept boutique in an artists' neighborhood. The secret is knowing how to choose the places that truly suit you.
blog_dubai_shopping_guide_p_12: The first thing that comes to mind when thinking about shopping in Dubai is the malls. And honestly? Rightfully so. The Dubai Mall is not just a mall — it's an entire city. With over 1,200 stores, a massive aquarium, an ice skating rink, and a world-class food court, you can spend a whole day here and still not see everything. Our tip: arrive early in the morning when the stores open, and plan in advance which shops interest you. Don't try to cover everything — it's simply impossible.
blog_dubai_shopping_guide_p_13: Beyond the Dubai Mall, we highly recommend visiting the Mall of the Emirates as well, which offers a slightly more relaxed yet equally indulgent atmosphere, with the famous Ski Dubai. And for those seeking true luxury, the Wafi Mall is the next stop — breathtaking Egyptian design, exclusive boutique stores, and a much more intimate atmosphere.
blog_dubai_shopping_guide_p_14: After indulging in the malls, it's time to dive into a completely different experience. The Gold Souk, Spice Souk, and Perfume Souk in Old Dubai offer an authentic glimpse into the region's traditional trading world. Our recommendation: head there in the afternoon, when temperatures drop slightly and the atmosphere becomes more magical. Don't hesitate to bargain — it's an integral part of the experience.
blog_dubai_shopping_guide_p_15: The Textile Souk in Bur Dubai is a hidden gem that not many know about. Here you can find stunning fabrics from around the world, and sometimes even have a garment custom-made within just a few days. It's recommended to arrive with a picture of the design you want — the local tailors are remarkably skilled.
blog_dubai_shopping_guide_p_16: Dubai in recent years is not just about major brands — the local fashion scene is flourishing. In areas like Alserkal or the Design District, you'll find young Emirati and international designers offering unique pieces you won't find anywhere else. This is also the perfect place to combine shopping with culture, with art galleries and stylish cafes around every corner.
blog_dubai_shopping_guide_p_17: In summary, the key to successful shopping in Dubai is early planning, blending different worlds, and plenty of curiosity. Don't try to do everything — instead, choose a few destinations that speak to you, give yourself time to stroll at leisure, and remember that the experience itself is part of the purchase. Happy shopping!

dubai_must_see_attractions_p_14: Dubai is a city of stunning contrasts – gleaming skyscrapers alongside traditional markets, white beaches and endless desert. Whether you're looking for a romantic getaway, a family adventure, or a luxury experience, Dubai offers a wealth of attractions suited to every type of traveler. In this guide, we've compiled the must-see attractions, with tips to help you choose the perfect experiences for you.
dubai_must_see_attractions_p_15: You can't talk about Dubai without mentioning the Burj Khalifa, the tallest building in the world. The ascent to the "At the Top" observation floors offers a breathtaking panoramic view of the city, the bay, and the desert. It's recommended to book tickets in advance for sunset hours, when the city lights begin to twinkle and the experience becomes especially magical. For families, the upper floors are a thrilling attraction, and for couples – nothing beats a romantic dinner at the At.mosphere restaurant on the 122nd floor.
dubai_must_see_attractions_p_16: Dubai Marina is one of the most beautiful places in the city, and there's no better way to experience it than a private yacht cruise. The cruise passes between impressive skyscrapers, Bluewaters Island, and Ain Dubai. This is a perfect option for groups of friends, special events, or simply for those who want to indulge. We offer a direct link to book yachts through our WhatsApp – just send a message and we'll arrange the perfect experience for you.
dubai_must_see_attractions_p_17: Head out of the city to the golden dunes of the Arabian Desert. A desert safari includes a thrilling 4×4 off-road drive, camel riding, sunset photography, and a traditional dinner under the open sky. This is an experience that suits families, couples, and groups – everyone will find the magic in it. Don't forget to bring a camera: the desert sunset is one of the most beautiful sights in the world.
dubai_must_see_attractions_p_18: Global Village is a massive cultural and entertainment park featuring pavilions from dozens of countries, each with unique food, products, and shows. It's a great place for families and travelers who love discovering new cultures. You can wander around for hours, taste foods from around the world, and buy unique souvenirs. Please note that the park is open only during the winter season (October–April).
dubai_must_see_attractions_p_19: Aquaventure, the water park at Atlantis Hotel, is one of the largest and most impressive water parks in the world. With extreme slides, a lazy river, and a private beach, it's a perfect fun day for the whole family. Adrenaline lovers will enjoy the "Leap of Faith" slide that plunges through a glass tank inside a shark aquarium. You can also combine a visit to the stunning The Lost Chambers Aquarium.
dubai_must_see_attractions_p_20: The View at The Palm is located on the 52nd floor of Palm Tower and offers a breathtaking view of Palm Jumeirah island, the Arabian Gulf, and the Dubai skyline. This is a must-see attraction, especially in the early morning hours or around sunset. Suitable for all types of travelers, with an outdoor viewing area that allows for stunning photos.
dubai_must_see_attractions_p_21: Dubai Miracle Garden is the largest natural flower garden in the world, with over 150 million flowers in creative designs. It's a paradise for nature lovers and photographers, with flower arches, castles, and flower-covered structures. The place is colorful, cheerful, and full of positive energy – perfect for families, couples, and anyone looking for a bit of peace and beauty within the bustling city.
dubai_must_see_attractions_p_22: Dubai is a city that offers something for everyone – from desert adventures to luxury indulgences. Whether you're planning a short trip or a long vacation, these attractions will ensure an unforgettable experience. And if you need help with planning, the Sarah Laloum Tourism team is here for you – feel free to reach out on WhatsApp and receive personal and professional service.

blog_dubai_dining_nightlife_p_10: Dubai is not just a destination for towering skyscrapers and luxury shopping. Over the past decade, it has become one of the most fascinating culinary capitals in the world, blending cuisines from every corner of the globe alongside a vibrant and upscale nightlife atmosphere. Whether you are a dedicated food lover or simply looking for a romantic evening overlooking the mesmerizing view of the Burj Khalifa, the city offers experiences to suit every palate and every budget.
blog_dubai_dining_nightlife_p_11: From Michelin-starred chef restaurants to street food stalls in traditional markets, Dubai's food scene is a non-stop sensory journey. In this guide, we will reveal the hottest spots, insider tips for making reservations, and the right way to combine a perfect meal with an elegant night out.
blog_dubai_dining_nightlife_p_12: When talking about luxury dining in Dubai, one cannot ignore the restaurants located within the luxury hotels. The "Armani/Ristorante" at the Armani Hotel in the Burj Khalifa offers a refined Italian experience, while "Zuma" in Gate Village continues to be a pilgrimage destination for lovers of modern Japanese cuisine. For those seeking a local experience, "Al Fanar" restaurant offers authentic Emirati cuisine in a nostalgic atmosphere.
blog_dubai_dining_nightlife_p_13: Important tip: It is recommended to book weeks in advance, especially during peak tourist seasons. At Sarah Laloum Tourism agency, we are happy to assist you with coordinating reservations and securing sought-after tables.
blog_dubai_dining_nightlife_p_14: After dinner, Dubai comes alive with a world-class bar and club scene. The Dubai DIFC area offers a Speakeasy concept with artisanal cocktails, while the JBR beachfront transforms into an elegant open-air beach party under the stars. Don't miss the revolving bar at the Hyatt Regency hotel, offering a breathtaking 360-degree view of the city.
blog_dubai_dining_nightlife_p_15: For Israeli travelers, it is important to note that some entertainment venues enforce a smart dress code. Men are generally required to wear long trousers and closed-toe shoes, while women can enjoy full fashion freedom. This is an excellent opportunity to dress up and feel part of the city's glamour.
blog_dubai_dining_nightlife_p_16: One of the most sought-after experiences among our clients is combining a guided culinary tour of the old city area (Al Fahidi) with a private yacht cruise at sunset. The tour includes sweet and savory tastings alongside explanations of the history of Emirati cuisine, followed by sailing to Dubai Marina for a festive dinner on board the yacht. This is the perfect outing for couples seeking romance or groups of friends looking to celebrate a special occasion.
blog_dubai_dining_nightlife_p_17: We offer personalized packages including dedicated transportation, luxury vehicles, and a Hebrew-speaking host who will accompany you throughout the evening. Contact us today to plan your perfect night out.

blog_kosher_dubai_jewish_life_p_11: Dubai, the city of luxury and innovation in the Persian Gulf, has become in recent years a sought-after destination for Israeli travelers. Beyond the glittering skyscrapers, lavish shopping centers, and golden beaches, the city is home to a vibrant and fascinating Jewish community, offering a unique experience that blends ancient tradition with extraordinary modernity. Whether you are looking for strictly kosher food or simply cultural curiosity, Judaism in Dubai is flourishing in a way that surprises many.
blog_kosher_dubai_jewish_life_p_12: The roots of the Jewish community in the United Arab Emirates may not run as deep as those of other ancient communities in the Middle East, but its meteoric growth is a remarkable story. The community began to take significant shape with the signing of the Abraham Accords in 2020, which brought about normalization of relations between Israel and the UAE. Since then, Dubai has become a welcoming home for Jews from around the world — businesspeople, diplomats, tourists, and young families who have chosen to settle in the city.
blog_kosher_dubai_jewish_life_p_13: The community is managed under the organization 'The Jewish Community of the Emirates' (JCE), headed by Rabbi Levi Duchman, the Chabad emissary leading Jewish activity in the area. The organization provides for all religious and community needs, and its center operates from a magnificent villa that has become the hub of Jewish life in the city.
blog_kosher_dubai_jewish_life_p_14:
As of today, there are no active synagogues operating in Dubai,







You can contact Chabad Dubai for details +971 56 958 2971
blog_kosher_dubai_jewish_life_p_15: One of the significant challenges for those who keep kosher abroad is finding suitable food, but in Dubai the solution is easier and more accessible than ever. Thanks to the active Jewish community, several kosher catering services, restaurants, and food stalls operate in the city. 'Kikos Restaurant, Fantastico Restaurant, La Fournil Restaurant and more offer a rich menu of meat and dairy dishes under EAKC supervision on behalf of Chabad Dubai. The kosher restaurants serve fresh and high-quality food, ranging from traditional Middle Eastern dishes to contemporary international cuisine.
blog_kosher_dubai_jewish_life_p_16:
In addition, luxury hotel chains such as Armani, Address, and Atlantis offer kosher catering services on request, allowing guests to enjoy a lavish hospitality experience while fully observing kosher laws. Large supermarkets also carry a growing selection of imported kosher products, including cheeses, snacks, and frozen meat.







There is also a kosher supermarket located in central Dubai — Super Rimon — carrying kosher products from Israel and around the world.
blog_kosher_dubai_jewish_life_p_17:
Jewish life in Dubai is not limited to prayers and food. The community holds cultural events, holidays, and social activities throughout the year. During holiday events such as Rosh Hashana, Sukkot, Hanukkah, and Passover, large community gatherings are organized in which hundreds of residents participate. The Passover Seder, for example, has become one of the most sought-after events in the city, featuring a festive meal, a special Haggadah, and an extraordinary international atmosphere.







Holiday meals are intended for residents only.
blog_kosher_dubai_jewish_life_p_18: The community also runs a Jewish school for children, Torah classes for adults, women's activities, and more.
blog_kosher_dubai_jewish_life_p_19: Before your flight, it is recommended to coordinate all your religious needs in advance. Contact the local Chabad House or the Jewish community organization for information and assistance during your stay for Jewish needs, kosher venues, and special events. Bring basic religious items with you (such as a tallit and tefillin), although they can usually be borrowed on-site. Remember that Dubai is a traditional Muslim city, but it greatly respects all religions. Modest dress is appreciated in public spaces, especially when visiting religious sites. And finally, be prepared to be surprised — the Jewish community in Dubai is a living testament to the power of tolerance, progress, and cross-cultural connection.

blog_kosher_dubai_shabbat_guide_p_11: Dubai, the glittering city of the future, offers an extraordinary experience even for the tradition-observant traveler. With proper planning, you can enjoy everything the city has to offer — from luxury hotels to world-class attractions — without compromising on Shabbat values. This guide is designed to turn your Dubai vacation into a relaxing, indulgent experience tailored to a Shabbat-observant lifestyle.
blog_kosher_dubai_shabbat_guide_p_12:
The foundation of a successful Dubai vacation begins with choosing the right hotel. Look for hotels that offer rooms on lower floors or those that provide easy access to stairwells, especially if you prefer to avoid using elevators on Shabbat. Many luxury hotel chains, such as those in the Dubai Marina or Downtown area, offer concierge services that will be happy to assist with advance coordination of Shabbat meals and special requests. A hotel with a balcony overlooking the waterfront or a well-maintained garden can make your rest time truly magical.







Feel free to contact us on WhatsApp for help finding a hotel that suits your vacation needs.
blog_kosher_dubai_shabbat_guide_p_13: It is recommended to contact the hotel before arrival and coordinate all halachic needs. Dubai's hospitality teams are known for their courtesy and ability to accommodate special requests. You can order kosher food from the kosher restaurants for Shabbat and also request heating using a dedicated hot plate that you bring from home.
blog_kosher_dubai_shabbat_guide_p_14: A Shabbat meal in Dubai can be an extraordinary culinary experience. You can choose to order Shabbat meals in advance from the kosher restaurants, which provide strictly kosher catering and also offer the option of a hot plate to keep the food warm and fresh. Imagine a festive Shabbat dinner on a balcony overlooking Dubai's glittering skyline.
blog_kosher_dubai_shabbat_guide_p_15: If you prefer full independence, you can bring dry goods from home and purchase fresh vegetables, fruits, and dairy products locally. Dubai's large supermarkets offer an enormous variety of quality food products, including well-known international brands. Prepare a shopping list in advance, so you arrive at your apartment or hotel room with all the ingredients needed for Shabbat meals already in hand.
blog_kosher_dubai_shabbat_guide_p_16: Dubai is much more than skyscrapers and shopping malls. Shabbat is an excellent opportunity to discover the calm and green side of the city. Take a leisurely stroll along the Dubai Marina promenade, where the pleasant breeze and stunning views create the perfect atmosphere for rest.
blog_kosher_dubai_shabbat_guide_p_17: Advance planning is the key to a successful vacation. Make sure you arrive at the hotel on Friday as early as possible, so you can settle in comfortably before Shabbat begins. If you plan to use public transportation or taxis, keep in mind that activity in Dubai is not suspended on Shabbat, but you may wish to avoid traveling. Therefore, a central location that allows walking to key sites is a significant advantage.
blog_kosher_dubai_shabbat_guide_p_18: Golden tip: Equip yourself with a printed hotel map and ask the concierge about the location of the stairs nearest to your room. Dubai is a city that respects all cultures and religions, and you will find that your requests are met with courtesy and professionalism. With the right preparation, Shabbat in Dubai will not only be possible, but also a spiritual and indulgent experience of the highest caliber.

blog_kosher_dubai_jewish_holidays_p_10: Dubai has become an increasingly sought-after destination for Israeli travelers in recent years, and for good reason. The combination of uncompromising luxury, stunning beaches, world-class attractions, and geographical proximity makes it a perfect vacation option. But when it comes to the Jewish holiday season, many questions arise: Will I be able to maintain my traditions? Is there kosher food available? What does the holiday experience look like in a Muslim country? In this article, we answer all these questions and provide you with everything you need to plan an unforgettable holiday vacation in Dubai.
blog_kosher_dubai_jewish_holidays_p_11: One of the main concerns of Jewish travelers is the issue of kashrut. The good news is that Dubai is well-equipped to welcome tradition-observant guests. In recent years, with the surge in tourism from Israel and the Jewish world, several kosher restaurants under recognized international supervision have opened in the city. Beyond that, leading hotels such as Atlantis, Burj Al Arab, and others offer kosher catering services for private events and holiday meals.
blog_kosher_dubai_jewish_holidays_p_12: For those who prefer to cook for themselves, imported kosher products can be found in dedicated supermarkets, including meat, poultry, dairy products, and kosher wine. It is important to coordinate orders in advance, especially during the busy holiday periods, and to verify the level of supervision with the supplier.
blog_kosher_dubai_jewish_holidays_p_13: Dubai offers a unique experience for every holiday. During Passover, for example, you can find hotels hosting "Passover vacations" that include a lavish Seder night, strictly kosher food, and family-friendly activities. During Sukkot, some hotels erect magnificent sukkahs for guests' use, sometimes with a breathtaking view of the sea or the famous skyline. During Hanukkah, a festive candle-lighting ceremony sometimes takes place in the hotel lobby, and the overall atmosphere is warm and inclusive.
blog_kosher_dubai_jewish_holidays_p_14: It is important to note that while Dubai is a Muslim emirate, the authorities demonstrate impressive religious tolerance. Nevertheless, it is advisable to maintain relative modesty in public spaces and to avoid excessive public display of religious symbols, as is customary in any foreign country.
blog_kosher_dubai_jewish_holidays_p_15: Early planning is the name of the game. Demand for holiday vacations in Dubai is very high, especially among the global Jewish community. Therefore, it is recommended to book flights and hotels at least two to three months in advance. Check with the hotel regarding the availability of kosher services, the possibility of setting up a sukkah (if needed), and the existence of organized prayer services. Some hotels even have a regular minyan during the holiday periods, but this should be confirmed in advance.
blog_kosher_dubai_jewish_holidays_p_16: Beyond the religious aspect, Dubai offers a wealth of attractions for the whole family. From breathtaking desert safari tours to massive water parks, from luxury malls to traditional markets — there is something new to do every day. Combine traditional holiday experiences with local attractions to create a balanced and unforgettable vacation.
blog_kosher_dubai_jewish_holidays_p_17: One of the great advantages of Dubai for the Jewish traveler is the sense of community. A vibrant Jewish community operates in the city, with a central synagogue, Torah classes, and social events. During the holidays, the atmosphere in the community is especially warm, and many Israelis report a sense of belonging and camaraderie. Don't hesitate to reach out to the local community before your trip — they will be happy to assist with information and guidance.
blog_kosher_dubai_jewish_holidays_p_18: In summary, a holiday vacation in Dubai is an extraordinary experience that combines the spirit of Jewish tradition with the grandeur and innovation of one of the most fascinating cities in the world. With proper planning, you can enjoy a kosher, joyful holiday filled with unforgettable experiences. Contact us today to start planning your perfect vacation.

dubai_hotels_marina_downtown_palm_p_11: Dubai offers a wealth of accommodation options, and choosing the right area can transform your vacation from a good experience to an unforgettable one. Each neighborhood in the city brings its own character, a different pace, and a unique target audience. Whether you're looking for a romantic getaway by the waterfront, a family vacation with all the luxuries, or an urban stay in the heart of the action – this guide will help you navigate between the leading areas.
dubai_hotels_marina_downtown_palm_p_12: If you want to be at the center of it all, Downtown Dubai is the perfect choice. The area boasts proximity to the Burj Khalifa, Dubai Mall, and the spectacular Dubai Fountain. Hotels here, such as the Armani Hotel or The Address, offer urban luxury and breathtaking skyline views. This is an ideal area for those who love shopping, chef restaurants, and entertainment into the early hours of the night.
dubai_hotels_marina_downtown_palm_p_13: The Marina is one of the most vibrant areas in the city, with a bustling promenade, gleaming skyscrapers, and an endless variety of cafes and restaurants. Hotels here, such as Grosvenor House or InterContinental, cater to a young and lively crowd. The great advantage is the proximity to JBR beach and attractions like the observation wheel "Ain Dubai". This is a perfect area for couples and groups of friends.
dubai_hotels_marina_downtown_palm_p_14: Jumeirah's iconic beachfront strip is a paradise for families. Hotels here, such as the famous Burj Al Arab or Jumeirah Hotels, offer direct beach access, private pools, and water activities for all ages. The atmosphere is more relaxed, with an emphasis on pampering and tranquility. If you're looking for a true all-inclusive beach vacation with all the amenities under one roof, this is the place for you.
dubai_hotels_marina_downtown_palm_p_15: The Palm is not only an engineering marvel, but also a luxurious resort destination in its own right. Hotels on the island, such as Atlantis and One&Only, offer an experience of complete escape within the city. You can enjoy private beaches, world-class chef restaurants, and attractions like the Aquaventure waterpark. This is an ideal area for those who prefer to stay within the hotel complex and enjoy everything it has to offer.
dubai_hotels_marina_downtown_palm_p_16: Choosing the right area depends entirely on the style of vacation you envision. In Dubai, every area is a world of its own, and the magic is in finding the one that speaks to you. Contact us on WhatsApp and we'll be happy to help you find the perfect hotel to match your Dubai dream.

Dubai is a vast and impressive city, offering visitors countless attractions, stunning beaches, magnificent malls, and vibrant entertainment districts. But how do you get around all these places smartly, without wasting precious time in traffic or paying a fortune on taxis? In this guide, we'll cover all the options available to the Israeli traveler, from the metro to luxury car rentals, so you can plan your trip like a pro.

Dubai's Metro is one of the most advanced public transportation systems in the world. It consists of two main lines – the Red Line and the Green Line – covering most of the city's central areas, including the airport, Dubai Marina, Burj Khalifa, and Dubai Mall. The carriages are air-conditioned, clean, and well-equipped, and there are even separate carriages for women and children. Prices are very affordable, and you can purchase a "Nol" Card (Nol Card) that is pre-loaded and can also be used for payment on buses and in parking lots.

The great advantage of the Metro is its ability to bypass the heavy rush-hour traffic. During peak hours, a ride from the city center to the Marina can take less than half an hour, while by taxi or private car the time may double. However, it's important to remember that the Metro doesn't reach everywhere – areas like Palm Jumeirah or Al Maha Desert require supplementary transportation.

Taxis in Dubai are an experience in themselves. The city's taxi fleet includes thousands of modern, air-conditioned, and regulated vehicles, with courteous English-speaking drivers. Fares are regulated and considered relatively affordable for a luxury city like Dubai – an average ride within the city will cost between 20 and 80 shekels. You can book a taxi through the Careem app (the local equivalent of Uber), or simply hail one on the street.

For those seeking a more luxurious experience, Dubai also offers a premium taxi fleet including Lexus, Mercedes, and even Tesla vehicles. The price is higher, of course, but for a short ride from the airport to a five-star hotel, it can be the perfect start to a vacation. Important tip: it's worth downloading the app in advance and making sure you have a working internet connection, as some drivers prefer navigation through the app.

If you're planning a trip that includes rides outside the city – for example to Abu Dhabi, the Hajar Mountains, or the remote beaches of Ras Al Khaimah – renting a car is the right choice. The roads in Dubai are wide and well-maintained, and the signs are clear even for those who don't read Arabic. Car rental prices are relatively reasonable, especially if you book in advance, and fuel costs are significantly lower than in Israel.

It's important to pay attention to a few points: First, driving in Dubai requires a valid international driver's license. Second, the city's fines system is very strict – illegal parking or excessive speeding can be very costly. And finally, it's worth considering renting a car with built-in navigation or using the Waze app, which works well in the UAE as well. We offer a particularly affordable car rental service for Israelis – (coming soon)

Dubai's bus system covers the areas the Metro doesn't reach, offering air-conditioned rides at very low prices. The buses operate at high frequency, and many of them include information screens and phone chargers. However, travel times may be longer due to traffic, so it's recommended to use buses mainly during off-peak hours or for short trips.

Another option worth knowing about is the Tram in the Dubai Marina area – a light rail of sorts that connects the Marina, JBR Beach, and the Blue Island. The tram ride is pleasant and fast, and it integrates well with the Metro and bus stations in the same area. Here too, the Nol card serves as the only means of payment.

One of the most magical ways to get around Dubai is by water. The traditional Abra boats cross the canal (Dubai Creek) and connect the Bur Dubai area to Deira, at a cost of one dirham per ride. This is not just a mode of transportation but also a tourist attraction in its own right, offering a glimpse into old and authentic Dubai.

For a more luxurious experience, you can rent a private yacht or join a group cruise along the coastline. We offer affordable yacht cruise packages that include stops at breathtaking viewpoints, with the option of a chef's meal on deck. This is the perfect way to combine getting around with an unforgettable luxury experience.

In summary, getting around Dubai can be simple, comfortable, and enjoyable if planned correctly. Whether you choose the fast Metro, a pampering taxi, a rental car, or even a yacht cruise – the main thing is to match the mode of transportation to your travel itinerary. Dubai has long since ceased to be just a stopover destination. For the Israeli traveler, it has become the perfect vacation spot combining accessible luxury, unforgettable experiences, and an exceptionally short flight. But planning a first holiday in this glamorous city can be a little overwhelming — there are so many options, attractions, and hotels that it's hard to know where to start. In this guide, we'll walk you through step by step in building a smart, enjoyable, and luxurious vacation — one that fits your exact pace and style.

The most recommended season to visit Dubai is between November and March, when the weather is pleasant and comfortable — temperatures of 24–28 degrees during the day, perfect for strolling outdoors, beach days, and poolside relaxation. The summer months (June through September) are very hot, but hotels offer particularly attractive prices, and all indoor attractions are wonderfully air-conditioned. If you're flexible with dates, try to avoid the Ramadan period if you're looking for a full dining and entertainment experience during the day.

Dubai offers an enormous variety of hotels — from grand beachfront resorts to intimate boutiques with canal views. Our recommendation: define what matters most to you. A family vacation? Look for a hotel with a kids' club and a variety of pools. A couples' getaway? A hotel with a luxury spa and chef restaurants will do the trick. At Sarah Laloum Tourism, we specialize in personally matching hotels to your needs — and offer direct booking via WhatsApp, so you can get an immediate response and great prices.

Dubai is packed with amazing attractions, but on your first trip it's worth focusing on the classics. The Burj Khalifa is of course a must – it's recommended to book tickets in advance for the At The Top observation deck. A private yacht cruise in the Marina is a perfect way to experience the skyline from the water, and we offer affordable cruise packages with the option of a chef's meal on board. Another highlight is the Global Village – a massive cultural park with stalls, shows, and food from around the world, open only during the winter months. Don't miss the desert safari with a dinner under the stars – an experience that connects you to the authentic and magical side of the Emirates.

Getting around Dubai is especially convenient. It's recommended to combine taxis (relatively affordable and available on every corner) with the modern Metro that reaches all central areas. A Nol Card will allow you to travel easily on all public transportation. Regarding dress code – Dubai is a modern and liberal city, but it's recommended to respect the local culture: in malls and restaurants you can dress freely, but when entering mosques or traditional areas, modest dress is required. Additionally, note that visas for Israelis are easily obtained at the airport, so there's no need to arrange anything in advance.

Sarah Laloum Tourism is a luxury tourism platform created especially for the Israeli traveler. We offer a One-Stop-Shop service – from booking a hotel with the click of a button on WhatsApp, to tickets for top attractions, local SIM card referrals, and transportation services. Our team knows Dubai inside and out and will be happy to build a personalized itinerary for you, including future services such as car rentals and babysitting services. A first vacation in Dubai is an exciting experience – and we're here to make it unforgettable.
